name: Memory Mesh
description: Portable semantic memory system for AI agents with automatic Layer 0 sanitization, local embeddings, and YAMO workflow integration. Enables agents to learn from past executions through pattern recognition.
longDescription:
Memory Mesh combines LanceDB vector storage, local ONNX embeddings (Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2), and a deterministic 6-stage content scrubber to create a self-improving semantic memory system for AI agents.

Key capabilities:
- **Persistent Vector Storage**: LanceDB with 384-dimensional semantic search
- **Layer 0 Scrubber**: Deterministic 6-stage preprocessing (structural cleaning → semantic filtering → normalization → chunking → annotation → validation)
- **Local Embeddings**: 100% local operation using ONNX (no API keys required)
- **YAMO Skills**: Includes yamo-super v2.1.0 workflow system with automatic memory integration
- **Pattern Recognition**: Learns from workflow executions, bug resolutions, and code reviews
- **Self-Improving**: Future workflows benefit from historical patterns

Memory integration at 5 critical workflow stages:
1. WorkflowOrchestrator: Searches similar past workflows for context-aware decisions
2. DocumentationAgent: Stores design summaries with commit metadata
3. DebuggingAgent: Retrieves similar bugs and stores resolutions
4. CodeReviewAgent: Stores quality metrics and outcomes
5. WorkflowMemoryStore: Aggregates complete execution patterns

Architecture:
- Scrubber-first design (clean before embedding for consistency)
- Multi-provider fallback (Local ONNX → Ollama → OpenAI → Cohere)
- Dual-cache strategy (embedding LRU + query TTL)
- YAMO protocol compliant (structured logging, meta-reasoning, graceful degradation)

skills.name: yamo-super
skills.description: Comprehensive AI coding workflow system with brainstorming, git worktree isolation, implementation planning, TDD, debugging, and code review - all with automatic memory learning

skills.name: scrubber
skills.description: Content sanitization using Layer 0 Scrubber principles (removes PII, boilerplate, normalizes structure)

tools.name: memory_mesh.js
tools.description: CLI interface for memory operations (store, search)

commands.name: store
commands.description: Store content with metadata in semantic memory

steps: Ready to use: /yamo-super in Claude Code

features.name: Layer 0 Scrubber
features.description: 6-stage deterministic preprocessing: structural → semantic → normalize → chunk → annotate → validate

features.description: 100% local operation using ONNX (Xenova/all-MiniLM-L6-v2), no API keys required

features.name: Automatic Memory Learning
features.description: Workflows automatically store and retrieve execution patterns for optimization

features.name: Pattern Recognition

features.name: Multi-Provider Fallback
features.description: Local ONNX → Ollama → OpenAI → Cohere embedding chain for reliability

useCases.title: Workflow Optimization
useCases.description: yamo-super learns from past executions: searches similar workflows before starting, adapts proven task breakdowns, stores complete execution patterns
useCases.example: Execute mode workflows learn that 'npm installable features' take ~45min, 5 tasks, with high success rate when following proven structure

useCases.title: Accelerated Debugging
useCases.description: Searches for similar error signatures before debugging, retrieves past solutions, stores resolutions for future reference
useCases.example: TypeError search finds similar bug (score: 1.36) with known resolution: 'Added initialization check'

useCases.title: Design Pattern Library
useCases.description: Stores validated designs with architecture, components, and commit metadata for reuse across projects
useCases.example: NPM installable skills system design stored with metadata enables similar packaging features to reference proven approaches

useCases.title: Code Review Learning
useCases.description: Stores review outcomes with quality ratings, issue counts, and approval status to inform quality standards
useCases.example: Past reviews establish quality baselines that future reviews can reference and maintain

useCases.title: Cross-Session Knowledge
useCases.description: Semantic memory persists across sessions, enabling long-term learning and continuous improvement
useCases.example: Memories survive restarts, allowing agents to build on past work weeks or months later

features: Error sanitization (redacts tokens, API keys, JWTs)
Local-first operation (no data leaves machine by default)

examples: Search workflows: node tools/memory_mesh.js search 'workflow execute npm' 3

changes: Added automatic memory integration to yamo-super skill
